questions.q: By what affectionate name was Matangini Hazra known?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was lovingly called Gandhi Buri, meaning the 'old Gandhian woman'.

questions.q: During which major movement did Matangini Hazra lead her famous procession?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra led a procession during the Quit India Movement of 1942.

questions.q: In which year was Matangini Hazra shot while leading a procession?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was shot in 1942 during the Quit India agitation.

questions.q: What was Matangini Hazra carrying when she was shot?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was shot while carrying the national flag at the head of the procession.

questions.q: In which region of India did Matangini Hazra live and carry out her activism?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was a peasant woman of Bengal who took part in the freedom struggle there.

questions.q: What was Matangini Hazra's social background?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was an elderly peasant woman, which made her devotion to the cause especially admired.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra's nickname 'Gandhi Buri' reflected her devotion to whom?
questions.explanation: She was called Gandhi Buri because of her deep devotion to Mahatma Gandhi and his ideals.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra is chiefly remembered for which quality?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra is remembered for her courage and martyrdom while carrying the flag.

questions.q: Even after being shot, what did Matangini Hazra reportedly continue to do?
questions.explanation: Accounts say she kept the national flag held high even as she was being fired upon, refusing to let it fall.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra is honoured as one of the notable women martyrs of which struggle?
questions.explanation: She is honoured as one of the women martyrs of the Indian freedom struggle.

questions.q: In which district of Bengal did Matangini Hazra lead her fatal procession in 1942?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra led the procession at Tamluk in the Midnapore district of Bengal.

questions.q: Around what age was Matangini Hazra when she was martyred in 1942?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was about 72 years old when she was shot, having been born around 1870.

questions.q: The procession Matangini Hazra led aimed to take control of which colonial institution?
options: The Tamluk police station and courts
questions.explanation: The procession aimed to seize the Tamluk police station and related government offices as part of Quit India action.

questions.q: In which year was Matangini Hazra born?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was born around 1870 in a poor peasant family in Midnapore district.

questions.q: Before Quit India, Matangini Hazra had taken part in which earlier 1930 campaign?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra joined the Civil Disobedience Movement in 1930, taking part in salt-law defiance.

questions.q: The short-lived parallel national government formed in her district after 1942 was called what?
questions.explanation: After the 1942 upsurge, revolutionaries set up the Tamralipta Jatiya Sarkar, a parallel national government in the Tamluk region.

questions.q: On what date in 1942 was Matangini Hazra shot dead?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was shot leading the Tamluk procession on 29 September 1942.

questions.q: What slogan was Matangini Hazra reportedly raising as she advanced and was shot?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra is said to have kept chanting 'Vande Mataram' as she led the procession and was fired upon.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra had also participated in protests against which colonial tax in her district?
questions.explanation: She took part in the movement against the oppressive chowkidari tax in Midnapore in the 1930s.

questions.q: How many bullets, by most accounts, struck Matangini Hazra as she refused to drop the flag?
questions.explanation: Most accounts record that she was struck by three bullets yet continued holding the flag until she fell.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra's life of hardship included being widowed at an early age. What had her family circumstances been?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra came from a poor family, was married young and widowed early, before devoting herself to the freedom movement.

questions.q: What is Matangini Hazra widely regarded as having become through her death in 1942?
options: A martyr of the Quit India Movement
questions.explanation: Her death made her a celebrated martyr of the Quit India Movement, especially honoured in Bengal.

questions.q: In which Bengal village was Matangini Hazra born around 1870?
questions.explanation: Matangini Hazra was born in the village of Hogla in the Tamluk area of Midnapore district.

questions.q: A statue of Matangini Hazra erected in 1977 stands in which city, a notable early honour for a woman freedom fighter there?
questions.explanation: A statue of Matangini Hazra was installed in Kolkata in 1977, among the first statues of a woman in the city's Maidan area.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra's procession on the fatal day was part of a coordinated plan to occupy several government buildings in which town?
questions.explanation: The procession was part of a plan to take over the courts and police station at Tamluk during the Quit India upsurge.

questions.q: In 2002 India honoured Matangini Hazra by issuing what in her memory?
questions.explanation: The Indian postal department issued a commemorative postage stamp honouring Matangini Hazra in 2002.

questions.q: The parallel government of the Tamluk region that emerged in the wake of the 1942 movement lasted until roughly when?
questions.explanation: The Tamralipta Jatiya Sarkar functioned from late 1942 until it was dissolved on Gandhi's advice in 1944.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra is sometimes compared in spirit to which other woman flag-bearer of an earlier era of Indian resistance?
questions.explanation: Her fearless death holding the flag has led to comparisons with Rani Lakshmibai, the warrior queen of Jhansi.

questions.q: Matangini Hazra's name has been given to a residential hall or institutions in West Bengal chiefly to honour what?
options: Her martyrdom and service to the nation
questions.explanation: Colleges, halls and institutions in West Bengal bear her name to commemorate her martyrdom and devotion to the freedom struggle.

questions.q: Which fellow Midnapore woman activist is also remembered alongside Matangini Hazra for the 1942 movement in the region?
options: Bimala Pratidevi (and other Tamluk women volunteers)
questions.explanation: Women volunteers of the Tamluk movement, remembered alongside Matangini Hazra, took active part in the 1942 agitation in Midnapore.
